normalized_title: Planning Bulletin 33/2017: Rights-of-way or Laneways in Established Areas
scope_summary: WAPC policy, practice and procedures for residential and commercial development and subdivision (including strata and survey-strata) adjoining existing private and public rights-of-way (laneways) in established areas across WA. It explains legal rights to use private rights-of-way (s 167A Transfer of Land Act 1893), sets expectations for sealing/draining and upgrading on redevelopment, gives design standards for width, length, truncations, parking, lighting and surveillance, and outlines dedication and widening mechanisms under the Land Administration Act 1997 and Planning and Development Act 2005. Greenfield laneways are excluded (covered by DC 1.7 and Liveable Neighbourhoods).

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum right-of-way width for vehicular access
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: rights-of-way used for vehicular access (per Liveable Neighbourhoods); widening to 6 m may be required free of cost on subdivision/development

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum width for WAPC support of dedication
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: dedication of right-of-way as public road; vehicles can pass at low speed at 5 m
garages at right angles to a 5 m wide right-of-way (manoeuvring space)
either side of garage entrance onto right-of-way
rear lots with sole vehicular access via a right-of-way

key_numeric_standards.topic: maximum right-of-way length for dedication support
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: rights-of-way without alternative pedestrian access to the street
where two rights-of-way meet, a right-of-way meets a public street, or at bends of 60 degrees or more (per DC 1.7)

cross_references.cited: Development Control Policy 1.7 - General Road Planning (DC 1.7)
State Planning Policy 3.6 Development contributions for provision of infrastructure

quality_flags: Multi-column PDF interleaving scrambles sentence order in places (e.g. section 6.5/6.6 headings merged with body text)
Typical laneway cross-section diagrams (document pages 6-8) are images with no extracted content
instrument_aliases.json maps 'Liveable Neighbourhoods' to DC-009, but manifest DC-009 is Development Control Policy 2.2 Residential Subdivision - alias appears stale so manifest_id left null
